title: sorbus aria subsp. mougeotii
intro: Sorbus aria subsp. mougeotii, also known as Whitebeam, is a subspecies of deciduous shrub or small tree that belongs to the rose family (Rosaceae). Traditionally, the bark, leaves, and berries of Sorbus aria subsp. mougeotii have been used in herbal medicine to treat various health conditions, with reported medicinal properties including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The plant is believed to have health benefits such as soothing digestive issues, reducing fever, and aiding in the treatment of respiratory problems. Sorbus aria subsp. mougeotii can be prepared into herbal remedies including teas, infusions, and infusates, often in combination with other herbs to enhance its medicinal effects.
